name: Evidence Ledger AgentSite
description: A static landing page that shows how each core claim maps to a repo-verifiable artifact.
brief: Create a credibility-focused static landing page for a service where every marketing claim is paired with proof artifacts, QA evidence, contracts, and deployment receipts. Keep it static, no analytics, no payments, no fabricated quoted endorsements, no fake metrics.
heroHeadline: A static site where every claim carries its receipt
heroLede: Evidence Ledger shows how service copy can stay persuasive without inventing proof: each claim points to contracts, QA output, deployment receipts, or documented scope.
audience: Founders who need trustworthy service copy
Maintainers who need claims tied to real artifacts
Agents improving a static site without inventing proof
visualDirection: premium editorial dark interface with a clear evidence ledger band

sections.title: Claim discipline before polish
sections.body: The site favors specific, supportable claims over broad promises or borrowed credibility.

sections.title: Artifacts make the copy maintainable
sections.body: Future agents can inspect contracts, runbooks, QA scripts, screenshots, and deployment receipts before strengthening copy.

sections.title: Trust boundaries remain explicit
sections.body: The page does not add analytics, payments, live data, fake customers, fake metrics, or unsupported guarantees.

proofArtifacts.label: Site contract
proofArtifacts.body: .agent/site.contract.yaml defines allowed claims, required sections, and approval boundaries.

proofArtifacts.label: QA command
proofArtifacts.body: npm run qa checks contracts, claims, links, SEO basics, and the Astro production build.

proofArtifacts.label: Visual QA screenshots
proofArtifacts.body: npm run test:visual verifies desktop and mobile layouts and saves screenshots for inspection.

proofArtifacts.label: Deploy verifier
proofArtifacts.body: npm run verify:deploy checks the live GitHub Pages URL for expected content.
